Requirement for section 22(b) Exchange Visa
Exchange visa issued to a foreign under 25 years of age
An exchange visa may, in terms of Section 22(b) of the immigration Act 2002 as amended as be issued by the Director General to a foreigner – who is under 25 years of age and has received an offer
                                                                to conduct work no longer than one year: Provided that –
- the prospective employer certifies that the position exists, and has committed himself or herself to –
(aa) pay such foreigner remuneration which complies with applicable legal requirements:
(bb) provide for the welfare and the needs of such foreigner while in the Republic under the aforesaid visa: and
(cc) Report to the Director-General the failure of the foreigner to comply with the terms of his or her visa or to depart when is required:
- ii. such foreigner may not conduct work other than work for which the visa is issued:
- iii. such foreigner may not qualify for a permanent residence permit within two years after the expiry of the exchange visa, which requirement may be waived by the Director-General in extraordinary
                                                                    circumstances; and
- iv. no visa may be granted under this section if the offer to conduct work pertains to an undesirable work as published by the Minister from time to time in the Gazette, after consultation
                                                                    with the Minister responsible for trade and industry.
(2) An applicant for an exchange visa in terms of section 22(b) of the Act, shall submit –

- No fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- Payment can be made by credit card, debit card (except American Express)
- Only applicants with a New Zealand residential status can apply for a visa.
- All bank statements must be latest at the time of application, and must show a minimum positive balance per applicant

                                                                some cases may take longer.

## Visa Fees
- Visa fees are subject to change annually.
- A visa fee of NZD 40.50 will be requested. This must be paid only at the visa application centre in person and cannot be paid online. Please see Payment Options below.
- There is an additional service charge of NZD 86 (Inclusive of GST) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants.
- Payment Options - credit card, debit card (except American Express),
- No personal cheques or company cheques will be accepted.

## Documents Required

## Medical Treatment Visa
In terms of the Immigration Act No.13 of 2002, a Medical treatment visa may be issued to a foreigner who needs to undergo medical treatment in South Africa for a period in excess of 90
                                                                        days.
The  following documentation is required and criteria apply:
- Form
DHA-1738 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Uv1bfoSlAWsuJymgEDpw1/f30d5327afcd44a7068d7b35b599c3c6/dha-1738.pdf)
- Payment of the prescribed fee of NZD 48.00 (non-refundable) and an additional service charge of NZD 86 (Inclusive of GST) per application. Payment can be made by using debit or credit
                                                                            card.
- A valid machine readable Passport / Travel Document valid for no less than (180) days 6 months after the expiry of the intended visit with three (3) completely blank pages. Machine
                                                                            readable passports which have been manually extended are not accepted for visas and for travel purposes.
- One passport size photo - Specifications refer the link:
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3YozSGKKEnIYsUlDKiZ3Rd/459688121587345232ddecc3fb5dafac/photo_spes.pdf)
- Proof of financial means to cover day to day needs of persons accompanying medical permit holder, if any, in the form of:

- Recent 3 months bank statements stamped and signed by the bank
- An applicant for a medical treatment visa shall submit:

a letter from his or her registered medical practitioner or medical institution within the Republic confirming:
- that space is available at the medical institution;
- the estimated costs of the treatment;
- whether or not the disease or ailment is treatable or curable;
- the treatment schedule; and
- the period of intended treatment in the Republic;
- The details of, and confirmation by, the person or institution responsible for the medical expenses and hospital fees: Provided that in a case where the applicant's medical scheme or employer
                                                                is not liable for expenses incurred, proof of financial means to cover the medical costs shall be submitted;
- Particulars of persons accompanying such permit holder
- Proof of return or onward ticket (provisional bookings)

- The processing time for Medical Treatment is: 4-6 weeks
- Applicants must apply no longer than 9 weeks before their intended date of travel to RSA.
- Applicants should also note that processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.

DHA-1738 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3Uv1bfoSlAWsuJymgEDpw1/f30d5327afcd44a7068d7b35b599c3c6/dha-1738.pdf)
